summary.intent: Fix subSessionId not being populated when spawning Claude subsessions, then build a full pinning system allowing users to bookmark events, tool calls, and checkpoints.
summary.outcome: SubSessionId and parent session linking now work via env vars passed through AppleScript spawn. A complete pin system was implemented: DB schema (PinnedEntity + PinFolder), backend CRUD routes, a Pins screen with folder collapsing, and right-click context menus on all pinnable entities.

repo: AppleScript terminal spawn is fire-and-forget — no return value. Session linking requires env vars (GOSSAMER_SPAWN_OPEN_ITEMS, GOSSAMER_SPAWN_PARENT_SESSION) read in the SessionStart hook handler.
Claude Code passes its env vars to hook subprocesses, enabling the env var handoff pattern.
The session detail screen has its own ClaudeTurnCard component that bundles Stop event + tool groups — it does NOT use EventItem, so any UI added to EventItem won't appear on Claude's replies there.
Prisma schema lives at /Users/stephanostsoucas/gossamer/prisma/schema.prisma; client regenerated via build step.
DB migrations applied with `npm run db:push` (uses prisma.config.ts for the default DB path).
Frontend API types and fetch functions live in app/src/renderer/src/api.ts.
Navigation items and active route detection are in app/src/renderer/src/Layout.tsx; routes registered in App.tsx.

code.finding: ClaudeTurnCard is the render component for Claude's responses in the session screen — separate from EventItem. Must wire context menus/overlays here independently.
Hook returns { onContextMenu, menuElement } — add onContextMenu to a container and render menuElement inside it to wire pinning to any component.
Portal-rendered floating menu, clamps to viewport edges, closes on outside click/right-click/Escape.

workflow: Env var handoff is a clean pattern for passing context from a fire-and-forget spawn to a later hook execution.
When adding UI affordances to list items, audit all rendering paths — the same data may be rendered by multiple independent components in different screens.
Context menu approach (right-click) is cleaner than hover icons for dense list UIs.

friction: Server restart via pkill failed silently; had to fall back to killing by port.
AssistantCard's background created a stacking context that obscured the initially attempted absolute-positioned pin button (z-index fix required).
Pin button on Claude's replies didn't work — root cause was ClaudeTurnCard bypassing EventItem entirely, requiring a second pass after user reported the bug.
Multiple edit attempts needed on CheckpointMessageItem.tsx and ToolUseItem.tsx, suggesting those files required careful targeting.
open_items: Folder assignment UI for pins is deferred — backend supports folderId but no UI to assign pins to folders yet.
PinButton.tsx was created but then the entire hover-icon approach was abandoned in favor of context menus; PinButton.tsx may be dead code.
No UI to create or manage PinFolder records yet.
Pins screen renders entity data inline but there's no navigation from a pin to the source entity (e.g., clicking a pinned event to jump to that session).
The 'pin' state in usePinContextMenu initializes without fetching current pin status from the server — the star won't reflect existing pins on load.
